rulings.Case Title: STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. MIGUEL TAPIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 22, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT State Farm's motion to enforce settlement is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for State Farm is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: DON TUCKER ET AL VS. THE TRAVELERS INDEMNITY COMPANY OF CONNECTICUT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Further Written Responses From The Travelers Indemnity Company Of Connecticut To Their Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Two
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 22, 2018, Line 9, PLAINTIFF DON TUCKER, MARTIN AGUILAR Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Further Written Responses From The Travelers Indemnity Company Of Connecticut To Their Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Two Plaintiff's motion to compel is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. I largely adopt the judge pro tem's well-reasoned report and recommendation regarding the five at-issue document requests. Nos. 25-28. The four named Travelers employees' performance reviews from 2010 to the present shall be produced. Privacy rights are not absolute, and the reviews are directly relevant to plaintiff's allegations that Travelers encourages claims adjustors to expeditiously deny insurance coverage. No 29. The request is overbroad. Documents setting performance criteria for the four named Travelers employees from 2010 to the present shall be produced. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: JACOB ELLISON VS. CHARIOT TRANSIT INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Joinder To Defendants Charter Bros Inc. And Renato Rodriquezs Motion To Consolidate Related Actions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 22, 2018, Line 5, CROSS COMPLAINANT CHARIOT TRANSIT INC. Notice Of Joinder To Defendants Charter Bros Inc. And Renato Rodriquezs Motion To Consolidate Related Actions The joinder is GRANTED. Charter Bros. Inc.'s motion to consolidate is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Cases CGC-18-565146, CGC-18-566940, and CGC-18-565145 are consolidated for all purposes. The court designates CGC-18-565145 as the lead case. The court will sign the proposed order, which now expressly reflects that CGC-18-566940 is also part of the consolidation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =302/rbu
